title: Swallowed Exceptions Hiding Security Events
description: Empty catch blocks or exception handlers that silently suppress errors can hide security-relevant events (auth failures, access denials), violating SOC2 CC7.2 monitoring requirements.

finding.message: Swallowed exception '{{token}}' in {{file}} at line {{line}} may hide security events.

references: At minimum, log the exception with context (user, operation, timestamp) before suppressing.
For security-critical operations (login, permission checks), propagate exceptions or return explicit error responses.
Use a catch-all exception handler at the framework level to ensure all errors are logged.
Consider alerting on high-frequency exception suppression patterns via your SIEM.
